heb.paragraph: "He remembers his covenant (bərîṭ) forever, the promise he made, for a thousand generations — the covenant he made with Abraham, the oath he swore to Isaac" (vv.8–9). The great history-of-salvation psalm. Give thanks, make known, sing, tell (vv.1–2). Remember his wonders (v.5). He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth (v.7). The story: Abraham called (v.6), covenant established (vv.8–11), patriarchs protected (vv.12–15 — "Do not touch my anointed ones"), Joseph sent ahead (vv.17–22).

notes.note: "Give thanks to the LORD, proclaim his name; make known among the nations what he has done." The opening commands: thanksgiving, proclamation, seeking. Worship as missional activity.

"He remembers his covenant forever — the covenant with Abraham. ‘To you I will give the land of Canaan.’" Covenant memory: God does not forget. The Abrahamic promise is the psalm’s theological engine.

"He called down famine on the land. He sent a man before them — Joseph, sold as a slave. Till what he foretold came to pass, the word of the LORD proved him true." Joseph: the suffering that preceded provision. The word tested Joseph before it vindicated him.

sections.header: Verses 23–45 — “That They Might Keep His Precepts”

heb.paragraph: "Praise the LORD (halləlū-yāh)!" (v.45). The history continues: Israel in Egypt (v.23), plagues recounted (vv.28–36), exodus (v.37), wilderness provision (vv.39–41), entry into the promised land (v.44). The purpose: "that they might keep his precepts and observe his laws" (v.45). History’s goal: obedience. Salvation is FOR faithfulness, not merely FROM trouble.

notes.note: "He brought out Israel, laden with silver and gold, and from among their tribes no one faltered." The exodus: no one stumbled. The entire nation walked out intact, enriched.

"He spread out a cloud as a covering, and a fire to give light at night. They asked, and he brought them quail; he fed them well with the bread of heaven." Cloud, fire, quail, manna. The wilderness provision catalogue.
"He gave them the lands of the nations. That they might keep his precepts. Hallelujah!" The purpose clause: the land was given for obedience. Gift and responsibility. Grace and law. Hallelujah.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: ’" Covenant memory: God does not forget…

ppl.name: Abraham
ppl.text: "He remembers his covenant forever — the covenant with Abraham…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVyou his servants, the descendants of Abraham, his chosen ones, the children of Jacob.ESVO offspring of Abraham, his servant, children of Jacob, his chosen ones!NIVRemember the wonders he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he pronounced,ESVRemember the wondrous works that he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he uttered,

rec.text: ""Give thanks to the LORD, proclaim his name; make known among the nations what he has done." The opening commands: thanksgiving, proclamation, seeking. Worship as missional activity."

rows.label: The Purpose — 105:23–45
rows.text: Plagues, exodus, manna. "That they might KEEP HIS PRECEPTS." Gift for obedience. Hallelujah.

hebtext.note: "He remembers his covenant (bərîṭ) forever, the promise he made, for a thousand generations — the covenant he made with Abraham, the oath he swore to Isaac" (vv.8–9). The great history-of-salvation psalm. Give thanks, make known, sing, tell (vv.1–2). Remember his wonders (v.5). He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth (v.7). The story: Abraham called (v.6), covenant established (vv.8–11), patriarchs protected (vv.12–15 — "Do not touch my anointed ones"), Joseph sent ahead (vv.17–22).

"Praise the LORD (halləlū-yāh)!" (v.45). The history continues: Israel in Egypt (v.23), plagues recounted (vv.28–36), exodus (v.37), wilderness provision (vv.39–41), entry into the promised land (v.44). themes.note: Covenant peaks at 10: the Abrahamic covenant drives the entire history. Mercy: God remembers for a thousand generations. The purpose: obedience to precepts.
